</thought>
<action>

<thought>
The agent is facing north. The cell directly ahead is a floor (`.`), while the left and right cells are lava and a wall respectively. Moving forward is the only safe action that progresses through the map.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is a wall (`#`), so moving forward is blocked. The left cell (west) is a floor (`.`), which is safe. Turning left will let me move into that cell next. Turning right would face east into a wall, so left is the better option.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing north, but the cell directly ahead is lava. The goal is located at the top of column 5, which is clear of lava from the agent's row upward. To reach column 5, I can turn right to face east, then move forward to the safe floor cell at (7,5). After that, I can turn north and proceed straight up to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>

MOVE_FORWARD

<thought>

<thought>

The agent is currently facing west (left). To head towards the goal, I need to face north. Turning right will change orientation from west to north. Then I can move forward safely towards the goal.
</thought>
